title: A Deal With Iran Requires an Iran that Can Make One
content: US President Donald Trump’s Venezuela gambit worked, to the extent that it did, because the state did not collapse. This might not be the outcome in Iran, because the Trump administration's war partners, Israel and the Kurds, have their own interests and ambitions, which do not necessarily include avoiding the fragmentation of authority.
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China’s Big Bet on Central Asia is Paying Off
content: The Western caricature of Chinese “debt-trap diplomacy” ignores the realities of China’s economic presence in Central Asia. What is emerging across the region is not dependency but interdependence, as Chinese foreign direct investment finances industrialization and mutually beneficial infrastructure projects.
creator: Djoomart Otorbaev
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe’s “Limited Responsibility” Model Must Go
content: In interventions at an informal European Council meeting and the Munich Security Conference, European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde recently explained exactly what Europe must do to secure its prosperity and sovereignty in the years ahead. The question now is whether European leaders will heed the call.
creator: Marco Buti
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: An AI Bubble Won’t Trigger a Financial Crisis
content: The AI boom may be speculative, excessive, and reminiscent of earlier episodes like the dot-com crash. But given the nature of the financing and the investments being made, the risks to the financial system are minimal, and policymakers should turn their attention to the impact of AI on the real economy.
creator: Lenny Mendonca
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can the US Afford Trump’s Iran War?
content: With the outlook for US employment and inflation already becoming increasingly grim, President Donald Trump's war of choice against Iran is bound to weaken public finances and the broader economy further, creating yet another reason for international investors to think twice about buying US debt. What ever happened to America First?
creator: Desmond Lachman
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Hungary and the Future of the EU
content: The European Union’s central challenge is to defend its members against external aggression, whether from the US or Russia, and Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s regime has long frustrated this effort. Even if Orbán is defeated in this year's election, EU leaders must take the right lessons from his 16 years of illiberal rule.
creator: Adam Bence Balazs
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Economic Magic of Equal Opportunities for Women
content: None of the 190 countries covered by the World Bank’s Women, Business, and the Law 2026 report provides women with the same legal environment as men, with the biggest gaps found in safety, entrepreneurship, and childcare. In developing economies, the costs in terms of growth and employment are considerable.
creator: Indermit Gill
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Kevin Warsh Is in for a Rude Awakening
content: For years, Kevin Warsh, Donald Trump’s nominee to serve as the next chair of the US Federal Reserve, has been staking out policy positions that would almost certainly backfire if put into practice. Fortunately, market conditions and the rest of the central bank's board will still have a say in monetary policymaking.
creator: Nouriel Roubini
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Turkey Wants in Iran
content: While avoiding protracted instability in Iran is vital to Turkey’s interests, so is ensuring that the Islamic Republic does not emerge victorious from the current war. Turkey's ideal scenario – a managed degradation of Iran’s ambitions and capabilities – might be best served by a Venezuela-style leadership transition.
creator: Sinan Ülgen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Stronger Work Ethic Won’t Fix Advanced Economies
content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz learned the wrong lesson on his recent trip to China. Advanced economies expand and remain competitive not through additional labor inputs but through capital deepening, technological progress, and total factor productivity growth.
creator: Jun Du
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Financial Insecurity, Not Immigration, Is Driving Populist Politics
content: Nearly a decade after Brexit and Donald Trump’s first election victory, populism is still often portrayed as a revolt by working-class voters struggling to keep up with economic change. But today’s electoral shifts reflect everyday forms of insecurity affecting a much broader segment of the population.
creator: Lorenza Antonucci
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Reclaiming Democracy From the Market
content: Meritocracy promised a fairer society, but instead it has deepened the divide between winners and losers and eroded the dignity of work. As AI reorders economies and concentrates power in fewer and fewer hands, the question is no longer whether democracy needs saving, but whether we can save it in time.
creator: Michael J. Sandel
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Iran’s Energy-Warfare Strategy
content: In its response to Operation Epic Fury, Iran appears to be exploiting the structural fragility of the global energy trade, since even a temporary closure of the Strait of Hormuz could have far-reaching global consequences. If energy infrastructure continues to be targeted as well, the risk will extend beyond prices.
creator: Avri Schechter
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Breaking Up Iran Would Be Catastrophic
content: It may suit Israel to foment separatist movements in Iran, but that doesn’t mean it serves the interests of the United States or its NATO allies. Western powers have a very poor track record with such strategies, and that would not change with a Balkanized Iran, which would produce one crisis after another.
creator: Michael Burleigh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran War Is Upending Global Energy Markets
content: The longer the US-Israeli campaign against Iran lasts, the greater the implications for the global energy system. Whereas short conflicts produce volatility, prolonged instability reshapes everything from trade flows to assessments of infrastructure risk and long-term investment behavior.
creator: Carolyn Kissane
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Makes America Strong?
content: In 2025, US net migration was likely negative, as the number of legal immigrants has been reduced in various ways. The overall effect will be to slow economic growth – perhaps by a lot – relative to what it otherwise could be.
creator: Simon Johnson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Birth of European Nuclear Deterrence
content: With French President Emmanuel Macron's announcement of a new doctrine of "forward deterrence," Europe is finally building a nuclear strategy that fully belongs to Europeans. The age of strategic dependence on the United States is drawing to a close.
creator: Antony Dabila
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Strengthening Women’s Rights Organizations Drives Economic Gains
content: Following foreign-aid cuts in donor countries, many developing countries have implemented austerity measures, to the detriment of women’s jobs, services, and protections. But donors and policymakers can deliver shared gains by investing in women’s rights organizations and embracing gender-based budgeting.
creator: Anisha Chugh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Long Road to War with Iran
content: Domestic political explanations for the Trump administration's decision to go to war with Iran risk obscuring deeper causes. In fact, the final decision appeared to be sudden only because the US, Israel, and Gulf countries' strategic trajectories had been converging for nearly a decade.
creator: Carla Norrlöf
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: When Is an Illegal War Morally Defensible?
content: US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu flagrantly violated international law by starting a war in Iran. But while historical examples of legitimacy trumping legality are few and far between, they do exist.
creator: Gareth Evans
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page
